title: Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters (Second Edition)
content:

The second edition of '''''Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters''''' is a sourcebook published for [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]] by [[West End Games]]. The authors were [[Mark Rein-Hagen]] and [[Stewart Wieck]]. The [[Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters|first edition]] was released in [[1990]], with the second edition (revised by [[Eric S. Trautmann]]) published in [[1994]].

''Tramp Freighters'' discusses various rules for roleplaying campaigns involving independent [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] characters, including rules for modifying [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], dealing with loan sharks, and trading legal and illegal cargo. It also includes a description of various planets, and personalities in the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] era. Adventure ideas and a five-adventure mini-campaign set in the Minos Cluster are also featured.

==Publisher's summary==
You are a free-trader... answerable to no one... hauling cargo for a tidy profit in your beloved ship, with your trusted comrades at your side... free from all danger, from all "entanglements"—Imperial or otherwise...

Sure it's not all fun and games, There are Imperial Customs officials to outwit, space pirates to battle, loan-sharks, corrupt portmasters, mechanical failures and a thousand other things which make your life interesting.

And somehow you keep getting mixed with that pesky Rebellion...

''Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters'' gives you information on Imperial regulations, ship modifications, and the Minos Cluster, an area filled with opportunity for freetraders like yourself.

Also described are the [[Quockran]], [[Gesaril (species)|Gesaril]], and [[Kari]] species.
